The reverse bevel incision is primarily designed to allow atraumatic reflection of the gingival margin, as seen in descriptions related to the modified Widman flap and other variants aimed at minimizing tissue trauma and ensuring proper surgical outcomes. Therefore, the correct answer is:
\boxed{A} Based on the detailed description and application of the reverse bevel incision in various periodontal surgical techniques, especially those involving minimized tissue disruption, the primary purpose aligns closely with allowing atraumatic reflection of the gingival margin.
